items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: ER now if cyanosis, confusion, air hunger, SpO2 <90%, silent chest, severe dyspnea, unable to speak, or poor response to reliever.

Avoid routine imaging/testing/drugs in typical BPPV.

items.Use_When: Use only for uncomplicated lower UTI; exclude pyelo, pregnancy, male, catheter, stone, recurrent/complicated UTI

items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Actrim [Trimethoprim 80 mg.+Sulfamethoxazole 400 mg.]
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: 2 tabs PO BID pc

items.Disease_Name: Group A streptococcal pharyngitis / streptococcal tonsillitis
items.Key_Clue: Sudden sore throat + fever + odynophagia + tender anterior cervical nodes/exudate; usually no cough/rhinorrhea/hoarseness/oral ulcers/conjunctivitis
items.Use_When: Use only if RADT or throat culture positive; if clear viral symptoms, route to viral pharyngitis and no GAS testing
items.Manual_Review_Note: diarrhea, rash
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Ibiamox 500 mg [Amoxicillin 500 mg.]
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: 1 cap PO BID

items.Key_Clue: Age 5–15 yr common; fever, sudden sore throat, painful swallowing, anterior cervical nodes, palatal petechiae/exudate; viral symptoms absent
items.Use_When: Use only if RADT/culture positive; child >3 yr with negative RADT needs throat culture backup before antibiotics
items.Manual_Review_Note: diarrhea, rash
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Amoxicillin pediatric product gap [Amoxicillin]
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: 50 mg/kg PO OD, max 1000 mg/day

items.Key_Clue: Fever, tonsillar exudate, tender anterior cervical nodes, absence of cough; RADT positive preferred
items.Use_When: Use only when GAS is confirmed/strongly likely; viral sore throat should route to viral pharyngitis
items.Manual_Review_Note: diarrhea, rash
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Ibiamox 500 mg [Amoxicillin 500 mg.]
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: 1 cap PO TID

items.Key_Clue: Painful genital vesicles/ulcers or first recognized genital HSV episode
items.Use_When: Use for suspected/confirmed first episode genital HSV; arrange STI testing/counseling
items.Manual_Review_Note: nausea, headache
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Acyvir 400 mg [Acyclovir 400 mg.]
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: 1 tab PO TID

items.Key_Clue: Recurrent genital HSV prodrome/early lesion; patient recognizes outbreak
items.Use_When: Use only for recurrent HSV; best if started within 1 day of lesion onset/prodrome
items.Manual_Review_Note: nausea, headache
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Acyvir 800 mg [Acyclovir 800 mg.]
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: 1 tab PO BID

items.Key_Clue: Severe fever/purulent discharge/facial pain >3–4 d OR persistent >10 d OR worsening after URI improvement
items.Use_When: Use only when CDC bacterial sinusitis criteria are met; otherwise route to viral URI/common cold
items.Manual_Review_Note: diarrhea, nausea, rash
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: AMK 1000 mg [Amoxicillin 875 mg.+Clavulanic Acid 125 mg.]
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: 1 tab PO BID pc

items.Key_Clue: Ear fullness / blocked ear / hearing loss with wax seen
items.Manual_Review_Note: Treat only symptomatic/obstructing wax or wax preventing exam; do not treat asymptomatic wax with adequate exam
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Docusate Earwax [Docusate Sodium 5 mg./1 ml.] — 5–10 drops affected ear; leave 15 min; BID x3–5d then reassess/remove || Aural irrigation [procedure] after softening if TM intact || Manual removal / ENT referral if failed or high risk
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Dewax Ear Drops [Docusate Sodium 0.5% w/v] — 5–10 drops affected ear; leave 15 min; BID x3–5d || Do NOT use ear candling/cotton buds
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs in-person eval if severe pain, discharge, bleeding, vertigo, sudden hearing loss, suspected TM perforation, diabetes/immunocompromise, or failed removal

items.Key_Clue: Child >6 months with obstructing wax, hearing concern, fever/ear exam blocked, or speech concern
items.Manual_Review_Note: Guideline target patient is >6 months; avoid home/self-irrigation in young child or high-risk ear
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Docusate Earwax [Docusate Sodium 5 mg./1 ml.] — 5–10 drops affected ear; leave 15 min; BID x3–5d then reassess/remove || Clinician irrigation if TM intact || Manual removal / ENT referral if child uncooperative, failed removal, high risk, or blocked exam
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Dewax Ear Drops [Docusate Sodium 0.5% w/v] — 5–10 drops affected ear; leave 15 min; BID x3–5d || Do NOT use ear candling/cotton buds
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs in-person eval if fever with unclear ear exam, severe pain, otorrhea, bleeding, vertigo, suspected TM perforation/tube, or failed removal

items.Disease_Name: Confirmed adult OSA with significant comorbidity
items.Key_Clue: Positive sleep test + heart failure/CAD/arrhythmia/refractory HTN/pulmonary HTN/COPD/stroke/renal failure/DM/depression/neuromuscular disease/opioid use
items.Manual_Review_Note: Comorbidity gates should bias toward PAP-first and specialist-supervised testing/titration.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Conservative treatment [non-drug] — weight reduction if overweight, avoid sedatives/alcohol, sleep hygiene || PAP therapy first-line [device] — CPAP/BPAP/ASV only as clinically appropriate and specialist-guided || Follow-up [monitoring] — reassess symptoms, residual AHI/RDI, leak, adherence, BP/cardiopulmonary status
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: CPAP if uncomplicated obstructive events and tolerated || Tier 2: BPAP if CPAP failure/intolerance, hypoventilation, sleep-related hypoxemia, or specialist indication || Tier 3: Surgery/OA/alternatives only after PAP assessment or if PAP refused/failed, with specialist evaluation
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs specialist/in-hospital pathway if unstable cardiac disease, COPD overlap/hypoventilation, neuromuscular disease, chronic opioid use, severe hypoxemia, central/complex apnea, stroke risk, or pregnancy hypertensive disorder.

items.Disease_Name: Confirmed adult OSA without significant comorbidity
items.Key_Clue: Positive sleep test; no major cardiopulmonary/neuromuscular/renal/stroke risk
items.Manual_Review_Note: Device/procedure row. No routine medication treats OSA itself.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Conservative treatment [non-drug] — weight reduction/exercise if overweight, sleep hygiene, avoid alcohol/sedatives before sleep || PAP therapy [device] — CPAP/APAP pathway with proper mask fitting and pressure titration || Follow-up [monitoring] — symptom response, residual AHI/RDI, mask leak, adherence, side effects
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: CPAP/APAP as main therapy depending on titration/resources || Tier 2: Adjustable mandibular advancement device if mild-moderate or PAP-intolerant and suitable dentition || Tier 2: Positional therapy if positional OSA || Tier 3: ENT/surgical airway evaluation if anatomic obstruction or failed device pathway
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs specialist eval if persistent sleepiness despite adequate PAP, residual high AHI, severe leak, complex/central apnea, PAP intolerance, high-risk occupation, or weight/health change requiring retest.

items.Key_Clue: New/progressive constipation with warning signs suggesting organic disease
items.Manual_Review_Note: Do not mask alarm constipation with repeated laxatives only.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Diagnostic/referral gate [non-drug] — evaluate secondary causes, medication causes, DRE, consider colonoscopy if alarm/CRC screening indication || Supportive only if safe [drug] — Forlax 1 sachet OD or lactulose 15–30 mL OD may be used short-term if no obstruction/acute abdomen while arranging evaluation
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Stop/adjust constipating drug if clinically safe: opioids, NSAIDs, CCB, diuretics, TCA, antihistamines, antiparkinsonian, calcium/aluminum/iron || Tier 2: Lab work only if clinical indication: glucose, TSH, calcium/electrolytes, CBC/FOBT as appropriate || Tier 3: Colonoscopy per alarm/CRC risk
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs in-person eval today if age >50 new constipation, blood in stool, alternating constipation/diarrhea, family history CRC, anemia/weight loss, acute onset/progressive change; ER if obstruction/peritonitis/severe vomiting.

items.Key_Clue: Hard stool/straining/incomplete evacuation or <3 bowel movements/week, chronic ≥3 months; mild or intermittent
items.Manual_Review_Note: Use fiber only with adequate water; may worsen bloating.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Lifestyle modification [non-drug] — fiber-rich diet, adequate water, exercise, toileting routine after waking/meal, toilet position with knees elevated || Mucilin Orange SF [Ispaghula Husk 3.5 g./5 g.] — 1 sachet in 150 mL cold water, drink immediately + follow with water OD–TID PRN || OR Fybogel Orange [Ispaghula Husk 3.5 g.] — 1 sachet in 150 mL cold water BID
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Forlax [Macrogol 4000 10 g.+Saccharin sodium 0.017 g.+Orange-grapefruit flavour 0.15 g.] — 1 sachet in water OD–BID || Tier 1: Duphalac 100 [Lactulose 66.7 g/100 ml] — 15–30 mL PO OD–BID || Tier 2: Milk of Magnesia C.B. [Magnesium Hydroxide 400 mg./5 ml.] — 15–45 mL PO HS/AM; avoid renal impairment || Tier 3: Dulcolax [Bisacodyl 5 mg.] — 1–2 tabs PO HS short course/on-demand if no response
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs in-person eval today if age >50 new constipation, blood in stool, anemia/weight loss, severe abdominal pain/distension/vomiting, constipation alternating diarrhea, family history colorectal cancer, acute onset/progressive change.

items.Key_Clue: Frequent hard stool/straining/incomplete evacuation; persistent symptoms affecting QoL
items.Manual_Review_Note: PEG has strongest evidence among conventional osmotics; guideline says conventional laxatives before new agents.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Lifestyle modification [non-drug] — fiber + water + exercise + toileting routine + toilet position || Forlax [Macrogol 4000 10 g.+Saccharin sodium 0.017 g.+Orange-grapefruit flavour 0.15 g.] — 1 sachet in water OD–BID for 1–2 weeks then adjust || Duphalac 100 [Lactulose 66.7 g/100 ml] — 15–30 mL PO OD–BID if PEG unavailable or pregnancy/hepatic indication fits
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Mucilin Orange SF [Ispaghula Husk 3.5 g./5 g.] — 1 sachet OD–TID if low fiber/no severe bloating || Tier 1: Milk of Magnesia C.B. [Magnesium Hydroxide 400 mg./5 ml.] — 15–45 mL PO HS/AM if renal function OK || Tier 2: Dulcolax [Bisacodyl 5 mg.] — 1–2 tabs PO HS short course/rescue; avoid chronic daily use || Tier 3: Combine osmotic + stimulant briefly if single agent inadequate, then step down to lowest effective dose
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs in-person eval today if alarm features, suspected bowel obstruction, severe pain/distension/vomiting, GI bleed, fever/toxic, dehydration, or fecal impaction not passing stool/flatus.

items.Key_Clue: Needs contraception with obesity, VTE, HIV, or valvular heart disease
items.Manual_Review_Note: Contraception products incomplete in workbook; gate only.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Safety screen [decision gate] — ask BMI, VTE history/acute VTE, anticoagulants, HIV stage/ART, valvular disease complexity, smoking/HT/DM, pregnancy plan || Prefer non-estrogen methods in high VTE risk/complicated valvular disease; use condom dual protection in HIV
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Slinda [Drospirenone 4 mg] — POP option if estrogen contraindicated; adherence needed || Tier 2: COC products such as Yaz/Yasmin/Meliane — only if no estrogen contraindication || Tier 3: Cu-IUD/LNG-IUS/implant/DMPA often preferred but product not in workbook; counsel/refer
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Do not use estrogen-containing CHC in history VTE, acute VTE, complicated valvular heart disease, or BMI ≥35 where risk outweighs benefit. Avoid IUD initiation in severe/advanced HIV until stabilized.

items.Key_Clue: Recurrent wheals/angioedema ≥2 times/week for >6 weeks, usually no clear cause
items.Use_When: Chronic urticaria without anaphylaxis; basic labs/trigger review needed
items.Manual_Review_Note: drowsy, dry mouth
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Zyrtec [Cetirizine dihydrochloride 10 mg.]
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: 1 tab PO OD

items.Key_Clue: Mucopurulent discharge, dysuria/urethritis, high PMN, Gram stain negative, NAAT CT positive or CT risk
items.Manual_Review_Note: Azithromycin failure higher than doxy in some settings; doxy default if not pregnant.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Doxylcap [Doxycycline 100 mg.] — 1 cap PO BID after food x 7 days || Abstain from sex 7 days after starting treatment and until partner treated || Partner(s) in last 60 days should be checked/treated
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Azithro 250 mg [Azithromycin 250 mg.] — 4 caps PO once for 1 g single dose alternative || Tier 1: Zithromax tabslet 250 mg [Azithromycin 250 mg.] — 4 tabs PO once || Tier 2: Ibiamox 500 mg [Amoxicillin 500 mg.] — 1 cap PO TID x 7 days if pregnancy/lactation option needed || Tier 3: Rintacin [Erythromycin stearate 250 mg.] — 2 tabs PO QID x 14 days, manual
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Avoid doxycycline in pregnancy/lactation. Evaluate for PID if pelvic pain/fever/CMT. Retest/TOC only selected; young patients may need 3–6 month retest.

items.Key_Clue: Burning/irritation, dysuria, yellow discharge without odor, acidic pH, abundant lactobacilli, yeast negative
items.Manual_Review_Note: No workbook vaginal sodium bicarbonate product.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Sodium bicarbonate vaginal douche [non-antibiotic] — baking soda 1–2 tbsp in warm water 1–1.5 L, douche 2 times/week every 2 weeks; expect improvement in 2–3 weeks || Stop unnecessary antifungal/antibiotic if diagnosis confirmed
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Soda Mint [Sodium bicarbonate 300 mg+Peppermint Oil 0.003 ml] — NOT for vaginal insertion; oral product only, do not substitute || Tier 2: Sodium bicarbonate 300 mg vaginal tablet protocol — product not found; manual compounding only || Tier 3: Reassess diagnosis if no response
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Do not treat as Candida/BV without confirmation. Evaluate if pelvic pain, fever, STI risk, pregnancy, bleeding, ulcer, or persistent symptoms.

items.Key_Clue: Obstructed defecation, excessive straining, manual maneuvers, sensation of blockage; poor laxative response
items.Manual_Review_Note: Guideline favors anorectal physiologic testing first in refractory constipation; biofeedback superior for defecation disorder.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Refer GI/colorectal [diagnostic] — anorectal manometry + balloon expulsion test first || Biofeedback therapy [non-drug] — preferred treatment if confirmed || Supportive constipation control [drug] — Forlax 1 sachet OD–BID or lactulose 15–30 mL OD–BID while awaiting referral
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Toilet position training [non-drug] — knees elevated/lean forward; timed toileting || Tier 2: Glycerin Suppositories For Adults [Glycerol (Glycerin) 91 g.+Sodium Stearate 9 g./100 g.] — 1 supp PR PRN if rectal outlet difficulty and safe || Tier 3: Surgical/pelvic floor evaluation only after specialist workup, especially rectocele/prolapse/intussusception
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs specialist eval; urgent if acute retention with severe pain/distension/vomiting, bleeding, prolapse strangulation, or suspected obstruction.

items.Disease_Name: Type 2 diabetes with chronic kidney disease
items.Key_Clue: T2DM with reduced eGFR or albuminuria
items.Manual_Review_Note: Requires lab-based dosing; not automatic OPD.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Renal safety review [monitoring] — check eGFR before metformin/SU; check albuminuria and BP || If eGFR ≥45: metformin may continue if tolerated || If eGFR 30–45: reduce metformin total dose max 1000 mg/day || If eGFR <30: stop/avoid metformin
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Prefer glipizide/gliclazide over glibenclamide if SU needed; still hypoglycemia caution || Tier 2: Galvus 50 mg [Vildagliptin 50 mg.] manual renal-dose review || Tier 3: Insulin/specialist if CKD advanced or uncontrolled
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Do not use metformin if eGFR <30. Avoid glibenclamide if eGFR <60. Avoid dehydration/acute illness metformin use.

items.Key_Clue: Diabetes age ≥40, or younger with risk factors; no clinical ASCVD
items.Manual_Review_Note: Ezetimibe missing; needs lab-based target follow-up.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Atorvastatin Sandoz 10 mg [Atorvastatin Calcium 10.34 mg. (eq. to Atorvastatin 10 mg.)] — 1 tab PO OD after dinner/night + lifestyle || If DM age ≥40 with 0–1 risk factor: target LDL-C <100 and ≥30% reduction || If DM age ≥40 with ≥2 CV risk factors: target LDL-C <70 and ≥50% reduction
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Atorvastatin Sandoz 20 mg [Atorvastatin Calcium 20.68 mg. (eq. to Atorvastatin 20 mg.)] — 1 tab PO OD if higher risk/target <70 || Tier 1: Atorvastatin Sandoz 40 mg [Atorvastatin Calcium 41.36 mg. (eq. to Atorvastatin 40 mg.)] — 1 tab PO OD if high-intensity needed and tolerated || Tier 2: Zimmex 20 mg [Simvastatin 20 mg.] — 1 tab PO HS lower-intensity substitute || Tier 3: Ezetimibe — manual if statin cannot be intensified; not found in workbook
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: High-intensity caution age >75, CKD stage 3b–5, prior nontraumatic ICH, major drug interactions, frailty, liver disease.

items.Key_Clue: Diabetes patient with low sugar symptoms or glucose ≤70 mg/dL
items.Manual_Review_Note: Workbook lacks dextrose/glucagon product rows; keep emergency meds manual.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Confirm glucose if possible [diagnostic] — Whipple triad: glucose ≤70 mg/dL + symptoms + improves after glucose rises || If conscious and able to swallow [supportive] — give fast carbohydrate per clinic protocol; recheck glucose || Review cause [safety] — insulin/SU dose, missed meal, alcohol, exercise, renal failure
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Mild conscious hypoglycemia → oral fast carbohydrate protocol || Tier 2: Severe/unable to swallow → emergency treatment, IV dextrose/glucagon protocol || Tier 3: Recurrent hypoglycemia → reduce/stop SU/insulin and reassess target A1C
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: ER now if unconscious, seizure, persistent confusion, cannot swallow, recurrent severe episode, sulfonylurea overdose, or no response to initial correction.

items.Key_Clue: Risk factor, high sugar, polyuria/polydipsia/weight loss, health check abnormal glucose
items.Manual_Review_Note: Do not auto-prescribe from a single unconfirmed asymptomatic reading.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: FPG / FCBG screening [diagnostic] — if FPG <100 mg/dL normal; FPG 100–125 mg/dL = IFG; FPG ≥126 mg/dL repeat another day to confirm DM || Symptomatic random plasma glucose [diagnostic] — classic symptoms + random glucose ≥200 mg/dL = DM || OGTT [diagnostic] — 2h-PG ≥200 mg/dL confirms DM if repeated/appropriate || A1C [diagnostic] — ≥6.5% can diagnose DM only if standardized lab; not preferred as broad Thai screening
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: If age ≥35 or risk factor → screen FPG/FCBG || Tier 2: If IFG/IGT → lifestyle + repeat follow-up || Tier 3: If classic symptoms/severe hyperglycemia → clinician evaluation, consider urgent referral
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: ER now if altered mental status, severe dehydration, vomiting, Kussmaul breathing, ketone/DKA concern, glucose very high with toxicity, pregnancy hyperglycemia, or hypoglycemia with unconsciousness.

items.Key_Clue: Fever + rash 1-8 weeks after new drug; facial edema, lymphadenopathy, eosinophilia/atypical lymphocyte, hepatitis/renal/lung/heart involvement
items.Use_When: Suspected severe drug eruption; especially aromatic anticonvulsants, sulfonamides, allopurinol, antibiotics, NSAIDs
items.Manual_Review_Note: Prednisolone/cyclosporine missing; avoid auto-RX systemic steroid without labs.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Stop suspected causative drug(s) immediately. Needs urgent in-person/hospital evaluation + CBC diff, LFT, renal function, UA ± CXR/other organ workup. Support only: Tylenol 500 mg strip 10s [Paracetamol 500 mg.] — 1 tab PO q6h PRN fever/pain if safe.
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Hospital/specialist systemic corticosteroid e.g. prednisolone 1-2 mg/kg/day until organ involvement improves; product missing from workbook || Tier 2: Antihistamine for itch only after evaluation: Zyrtec [Cetirizine 10 mg.] 1 tab PO OD || Tier 3: Cyclosporine/N-acetylcysteine reported options only specialist/manual
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: ER now + suspected DRESS because mortality risk and internal organ involvement. Do not rechallenge culprit drug; issue allergy card and record allergy.

items.Disease_Name: Dyslipidemia screening and ASCVD risk assessment
items.Key_Clue: Age ≥35, ASCVD/subclinical ASCVD, diabetes, CKD, suspected familial dyslipidemia, xanthoma/arcus young
items.Manual_Review_Note: Screening/triage row; no drug unless risk group matched.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Check TC, TG, HDL-C; calculate LDL-C = TC - HDL-C - TG/5 if TG <400 || Use direct LDL-C if TG ≥400 or LDL-C <50 || Calculate Non-HDL-C = TC - HDL-C if TG high/diabetes/obesity || Assess Thai CV Risk Score age 35–70 if no ASCVD
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: If LDL-C ≥190 → hypercholesterolemia_ldl_ge_190 || Tier 1: If clinical ASCVD → clinical_ascvd_secondary_prevention || Tier 1: If diabetes → diabetes_dyslipidemia_primary || Tier 2: If TG ≥500 → severe_hypertriglyceridemia || Tier 3: If low risk and LDL-C <190 without subclinical ASCVD → lifestyle first
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs in-person eval if xanthoma/arcus <45, premature ASCVD family history, TG very high, secondary cause suspected, liver/renal disease, pregnancy, or statin adverse effect.

items.Key_Clue: Ovarian cyst suspicious for endometrioma; cyclic pain or infertility
items.Manual_Review_Note: Do not auto-treat large/uncertain cyst as simple dysmenorrhea.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Confirm by pelvic ultrasound; assess size, malignancy features, fertility plan || If cyst ≥4 cm, uncertain diagnosis, fertility desire, nonresponse after >6 months medical therapy, or complications → gyne/surgical referral
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: If small cyst, confident benign, no fertility desire → COC/manual hormonal suppression may be considered || Tier 2: Yaz [Drospirenone 3 mg.+Ethinylestradiol 0.02 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD if COC chosen and no contraindication || Tier 2: Yasmin-21 [Drospirenone 3 mg.+Ethinylestradiol 0.030 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD if COC chosen || Tier 3: Dienogest/NETA/MPA/DMPA/GnRHa — MANUAL; products not found in workbook
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs gyne eval today/urgent if acute severe pain, rupture/torsion concern, fever, pregnancy, suspicious malignancy features, rapidly enlarging cyst, or cyst ≥4 cm.

items.Key_Clue: Dysmenorrhea impairing life, chronic pelvic pain, deep dyspareunia, cyclic bowel/urinary pain, infertility with pain
items.Manual_Review_Note: Hormonal options recommended by guideline, but several key products missing from workbook.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Gofen 400 [Ibuprofen 400 mg.+Polyethylene 400+Porpylene Glycol] — 1 cap PO q6–8 hr PRN after food during pain/menses || If contraception desired and no estrogen contraindication: Yaz [Drospirenone 3 mg.+Ethinylestradiol 0.02 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD cyclic or continuous, clinician-directed || Arrange pelvic exam + TVS/TAUS depending sexual history
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Ponstan 500 mg [Mefenamic acid 500 mg.] — 1 tab PO TID after food during menses/pain || Tier 1: Yasmin-21 [Drospirenone 3 mg.+Ethinylestradiol 0.030 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD cyclic/continuous if no estrogen contraindication || Tier 1: Meliane 21 [Gestodene 0.075 mg.+Ethinylestradiol 0.02 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD if COC chosen || Tier 2: Slinda [Drospirenone 4 mg] — 1 tab PO OD if estrogen avoidance needed, but endometriosis-specific evidence not guideline primary || Tier 3: Dienogest/NETA/MPA/DMPA/GnRHa/LNG-IUS — MANUAL; products not found in workbook
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs gyne referral if infertility, endometrioma suspected, severe/deep infiltrating symptoms, bowel/urinary cyclic bleeding, hydronephrosis concern, pelvic mass, poor response after 2–3 months, or hormone contraindication.

items.Key_Clue: After conservative surgery for endometriosis/endometrioma; does not currently desire pregnancy
items.Manual_Review_Note: Guideline supports OCP/progestogen/GnRHa/LNG-IUS; workbook has COCs/Slinda but not many endometriosis-specific agents.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Long-term hormonal suppression >6 months if no pregnancy desire, manual clinician selection || Workbook-supported COC option if no estrogen contraindication: Yaz [Drospirenone 3 mg.+Ethinylestradiol 0.02 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD cyclic or continuous
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Yasmin-21 [Drospirenone 3 mg.+Ethinylestradiol 0.030 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD if COC chosen || Tier 1: Meliane 21 [Gestodene 0.075 mg.+Ethinylestradiol 0.02 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD if COC chosen || Tier 2: Slinda [Drospirenone 4 mg] — 1 tab PO OD if estrogen avoidance needed, manual/off-guideline-specific || Tier 3: Dienogest, NETA, MPA/DMPA, LNG-IUS, GnRHa — MANUAL; missing workbook products
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Do not start estrogen-containing COC if VTE, migraine with aura, uncontrolled HT, smoking age ≥35, breast cancer, severe liver disease, pregnancy, or other estrogen contraindication.

items.Key_Clue: active anterior nosebleed / recurrent bleeding
items.Use_When: minor anterior epistaxis after initial assessment; hemodynamically stable
items.Avoid_When: posterior bleed, unstable vitals, severe trauma, anticoagulant complication, bleeding disorder, persistent heavy bleeding
items.Red_Flags_One_Line: ER now if unstable, posterior bleed suspected, major trauma, severe/recurrent bleeding, or cannot stop with compression
items.Notes_Internal: ADD NEW: Thai epistaxis guideline 2024 is procedural; this row is for clinic support/triage, not routine self-medication.
items.RX1_Order_Line: 1–2 sprays into affected nostril once; then firm nasal compression 10–15 min; may repeat once if needed
items.RX1_Duration: same visit / max 3 days if prescribed
items.RX1_Quick_Caution: short course; caution HTN/CVD; do not delay evaluation if heavy bleeding
items.RX2_Display_Name: Klean & Kare Saline เล็ก [Sodium Chloride 0.9 g./100 ml.]
items.RX2_Order_Line: gentle saline cleaning/moistening after bleeding controlled PRN
items.RX2_Quick_Caution: avoid forceful irrigation during active bleeding
items.RX3_Order_Line: 1 tab PO q6h PRN pain
items.RX3_Quick_Caution: avoid duplicate paracetamol; avoid NSAIDs if active/recurrent bleeding risk
items.SWAP1_Display_Name: Terramycin Ophthalmic Ointment [Oxytetracycline HCl 5 mg.+Polymyxin B Sulfate 10,000 units/1 g.]
items.SWAP1_Order_Line: clinician-only: apply thinly to nasal packing material as lubricant/antibiotic ointment if packing performed
items.SWAP1_Quick_Caution: procedure support; not routine nose self-application; product is ophthalmic ointment in workbook
items.SWAP2_Order_Line: 1 tab PO BID only if non-absorbable nasal packing placed and clinician chooses oral anti-staph coverage
items.SWAP2_Duration: 7 days; guideline allows 7–10 days after packing
items.SWAP2_Quick_Caution: manual review; penicillin allergy; not for simple epistaxis without packing

items.Source_Anchor: Epistaxis: topical decongestant for preparation; packing materials; ATB after packing; posterior packing admission
items.Manual_Review_Note: Oral antibiotic choice is not named by guideline; AMK row is a local workbook candidate only after packing.

items.Disease_Name: Febrile UTI suspected from fever workup
items.Key_Clue: Fever without source + abnormal UA: leukocyte esterase/nitrite positive or urine WBC ≥5/HPF
items.Use_When: Use when FWS workup suggests UTI; culture before antibiotic
items.Source_File: Needs eval/admit if age <3 mo, toxic, vomiting/dehydration, persistent fever, flank pain, bacteremia, or unable oral intake. Do not diagnose UTI from bag culture alone.
items.Verification_Status: Thai Pediatric Acute Febrile Illness CPG 2019 + workbook
items.Manual_Review_Note: SOURCE_VERIFIED_PARTIAL_PRODUCT_GAP
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Sterile urine culture required. If febrile infant/SBI risk, initial parenteral 3rd-gen cephalosporin preferred; product gap.
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Tier 1: Manual ceftriaxone gap [Ceftriaxone] 75-100 mg/kg IM/IV after blood/urine culture if febrile/SBI risk || Tier 2: Cefspan Suspension [Cefixime 100 mg./5 ml.] — oral step-down only when clinically improved and culture/sensitivity appropriate || Tier 2: Augmentin 457 mg [Amoxicillin 400 mg.+Clavulanic Acid 57 mg./5 ml.] — oral step-down if susceptible || Tier 3: Cifloxin 500 mg [Ciprofloxacin 500 mg.] — exceptional older child/no better option, specialist decision

items.Disease_Name: Suspected fecal impaction / rectal stool burden
items.Key_Clue: No stool several days + rectal fullness/tenesmus; possible hard stool in rectum; no acute abdomen
items.Manual_Review_Note: Do not recommend self large-volume colonic irrigation. Sodium phosphate enema caution in elderly/renal/heart/neuro disease.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Glycerin Suppositories For Adults [Glycerol (Glycerin) 91 g.+Sodium Stearate 9 g./100 g.] — 1 supp PR once PRN, wait ~15 min || Forlax [Macrogol 4000 10 g.+Saccharin sodium 0.017 g.+Orange-grapefruit flavour 0.15 g.] — 1 sachet PO OD–BID after rectal relief || Hydration + toileting routine [non-drug]
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Dulcolax [Bisacodyl 5 mg.] — 1–2 tabs PO HS short course if no obstruction and oral route OK || Tier 2: Milk of Magnesia C.B. [Magnesium Hydroxide 400 mg./5 ml.] — 15–45 mL PO once daily if renal function OK || Tier 3: Specialist/clinic rectal management if severe impaction, frail elderly, neuro disease, or failed suppository
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: ER now if severe abdominal pain/distension, vomiting, peritonitis, unable to pass flatus, fever/toxic, GI bleeding, suspected obstruction/perforation, severe dehydration.

items.Key_Clue: Heartburn symptoms with normal endoscopy/reflux burden or poor PPI response; often hypersensitivity/functional overlap
items.Manual_Review_Note: Do not present as default GERD row; use after refractory assessment.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Education/reassurance [non-drug] — explain non-acid/non-GERD mechanism after evaluation || Avoid repeated PPI escalation [safety] — reassess diagnosis || GI referral [referral] — consider pH-impedance/manometry if persistent
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Low-dose neuromodulator only after diagnosis/specialist plan; do not auto-RX from short OPD input || Tier 2: Relaxation/diaphragmatic breathing/CBT-type support || Tier 3: Treat overlapping IBS/anxiety/sleep only if clearly present
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs in-person/GI eval if alarm symptoms, new severe chest pain, dysphagia, bleeding, weight loss, vomiting, or persistent symptoms despite appropriate GERD trial.

items.Key_Clue: Rectal/core temp >38°C, no clear source, age 0-28 days
items.Use_When: Any fever without clear source in neonate; also premature infant corrected early period
items.Source_File: Cannot routine OPD. ER/admit now + neonatal fever has high SBI/meningitis risk even if well-appearing.
items.Verification_Status: Thai Pediatric Acute Febrile Illness CPG 2019
items.Manual_Review_Note: SOURCE_VERIFIED_ER_GATE
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Admit. Complete septic workup: CBC, UA, blood culture, urine culture, CSF analysis if possible. Start IV antibiotics.
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Tier 1: Manual cefotaxime IV gap + aminoglycoside IV/IM gap; consider ampicillin IV gap depending local Listeria/GBS/Enterococcus risk || Tier 2: Manual ceftriaxone generally avoid in neonate unless specialist context; use hospital protocol || Tier 3: Manual IV acyclovir gap if suspected meningoencephalitis/HSV

items.Disease_Name: Fever without source, infant 1-3 months, high risk
items.Key_Clue: Age 1-3 mo with fever and any high-risk criterion: toxic, abnormal exam, WBC outside 5-15k, band >1500, UA WBC ≥5/HPF, stool WBC ≥5/HPF, prematurity/comorbidity
items.Use_When: Any 1-3 mo infant not meeting low-risk criteria
items.Source_File: Needs in-person eval today/admit + young infant high-risk FWS. Do not use oral antibiotic as empirical SBI treatment.
items.Verification_Status: Thai Pediatric Acute Febrile Illness CPG 2019
items.Manual_Review_Note: SOURCE_VERIFIED_ER_GATE
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Admit or urgent pediatric evaluation. CBC, UA, blood culture, urine culture if UA abnormal, consider CSF. Start IV antibiotic if SBI risk.
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Tier 1: Manual cefotaxime IV gap or ceftriaxone IM/IV gap per guideline/hospital protocol || Tier 2: Manual acyclovir IV gap if encephalitis/HSV concern || Tier 3: If blood culture positive → occult_bacteremia_peds_gate

items.Disease_Name: Fever without source, infant 1-3 months, low risk
items.Key_Clue: Age 1-3 mo, fever >38°C, non-toxic, full-term/healthy, normal exam, CBC/UA low-risk
items.Use_When: Only if all Rochester/AAP low-risk criteria met and reliable 24h follow-up
items.Manual_Review_Note: GI upset, rash
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Tylenol Suspension [Paracetamol 160 mg./5 ml.]
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: 10-15 mg/kg/dose PO q4-6h PRN fever/pain

items.Disease_Name: Fever without source, 3-36 months, non-toxic, fever ≥39°C
items.Key_Clue: Age 3-36 mo, non-toxic but fever ≥39°C without clear source
items.Use_When: Needs CBC/UA risk stratification ± cultures; possible outpatient ceftriaxone if high-risk and follow-up reliable
items.Source_File: Do not give oral amoxicillin/azithromycin just for unexplained fever. Needs 24h follow-up if treated as outpatient. ER/admit if toxic, poor perfusion, dehydration, petechiae/purpura, altered mental status, respiratory distress, or unreliable follow-up.
items.Verification_Status: Thai Pediatric Acute Febrile Illness CPG 2019
items.Manual_Review_Note: SOURCE_VERIFIED_GATE_PRODUCT_GAP
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Do workup-first: CBC, UA; urine culture if UA abnormal; blood culture if fever >39°C or WBC >15,000 or clinician will give antibiotic; CXR if tachypnea/WOB or fever ≥39°C + WBC ≥20,000.
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Tier 1: Tylenol Suspension [Paracetamol 160 mg./5 ml.] — antipyretic support || Tier 2: Manual ceftriaxone gap [Ceftriaxone] 75-100 mg/kg IM/IV once after cultures if WBC >15k, uncertain toxic, or SBI concern and outpatient follow-up possible || Tier 3: If toxic or cannot follow-up → fever_without_source_peds_3_36mo_toxic_gate

items.Disease_Name: Fever without source, 3-36 months, non-toxic, fever <39°C
items.Key_Clue: Age 3-36 mo, non-toxic, feeding/playing, fever <39°C, no local bacterial focus
items.Use_When: Stable child with low fever and no source
items.Manual_Review_Note: GI upset, rash
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Tylenol Suspension [Paracetamol 160 mg./5 ml.]
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: 10-15 mg/kg/dose PO q4-6h PRN fever/pain

items.Disease_Name: Fever without source, 3-36 months, toxic appearance
items.Key_Clue: Toxic appearance/Yale score >10, poor perfusion, lethargy, poor response, dehydration, abnormal color
items.Use_When: Any toxic-appearing febrile child 3-36 mo, regardless WBC
items.Source_File: ER/admit now + toxic febrile child/SBI risk. Do not manage with oral antibiotic alone.
items.Verification_Status: Thai Pediatric Acute Febrile Illness CPG 2019
items.Manual_Review_Note: SOURCE_VERIFIED_ER_GATE
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Admit. Septic workup: CBC, UA, blood culture, urine culture if UA abnormal, CSF if indicated, CXR if respiratory signs/WBC ≥20k. Start IV antibiotic.
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Tier 1: Manual cefotaxime IV gap or ceftriaxone IM/IV gap per guideline || Tier 2: Add organism/site-specific coverage after source identified || Tier 3: If cephalosporin anaphylaxis and SBI suspected → specialist/hospital fluoroquinolone ± clindamycin pathway, product caution

items.Key_Clue: Mucopurulent discharge, urethral pus, high PMN, STI risk, Gram-negative diplococci
items.Manual_Review_Note: Ceftriaxone not in workbook; keep preferred regimen manual.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Preferred: Ceftriaxone 500 mg IM once — MANUAL, product not found in workbook || If CT not excluded: add CT treatment row || Abstain from sex 7 days after treatment and until partners treated
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Sixime [Cefixime 100 mg.] — 8 caps PO once for cefixime 800 mg fallback if ceftriaxone unavailable; manual || Tier 2: Doxylcap [Doxycycline 100 mg.] — 1 cap PO BID x 7 days if CT not excluded, avoid pregnancy/lactation || Tier 2: Azithro 250 mg [Azithromycin 250 mg.] — 4 caps PO once for 1 g CT alternative || Tier 3: Cephalosporin allergy severe → gentamicin 240 mg IM + azithromycin 2 g PO once, MANUAL
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: GC needs STI management, partner treatment, and follow-up. Needs urgent eval if pelvic pain, fever, pregnancy, disseminated infection, conjunctivitis, sexual assault, or cephalosporin allergy.

items.Key_Clue: Pregnant with DM risk or abnormal glucose; 24–28 week screening window
items.Manual_Review_Note: Do not auto-start routine nonpregnant T2DM regimen.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Pregnancy glucose screen [diagnostic] — FPG at first ANC; OGTT at 24–28 weeks per pregnancy protocol || Diet/exercise education [non-drug] || Refer OB/endocrine/DM clinic for pregnancy-specific targets and fetal monitoring
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: GDM controlled with diet/exercise → close pregnancy follow-up || Tier 2: If not controlled → insulin/specialist pathway || Tier 3: Metformin in pregnancy is manual/specialist only, not default auto-RX
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs OB/endocrine evaluation; ER/urgent if vomiting/dehydration, ketones, severe hyperglycemia, reduced fetal movement, preeclampsia symptoms.

items.Key_Clue: Typical heartburn/regurgitation without alarm features
items.Manual_Review_Note: PPI is cornerstone; alginate add-on supported. Keep short course for simple OPD unless chronic/severe indication.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Miracid [Omeprazole 20 mg.] — 1 cap PO OD before meal x 14–28 days || Gaviscon Advance sachet 10 ml [Sodium alginate 1000 mg + Potassium bicarbonate 200 mg + excipients] — 1 sachet PO after meals/HS PRN x 3–7 days || Lifestyle [non-drug] — avoid late meals 2–3 h before bed, weight reduction if overweight, head-of-bed elevation if nocturnal
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Nexium 20 mg [Esomeprazole 20 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD before meal x 14–28 days || Tier 1: Controloc 40 mg [Pantoprazole 40 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD x 14–28 days || Tier 2: Pariet 20 mg [Rabeprazole 20 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD x 14–28 days || Tier 2: Gaviscon Dual Action sachet 10 ml [Sodium alginate 500 mg + Sodium bicarbonate 213 mg + Calcium carbonate 325 mg] — 1 sachet after meals/HS PRN || Tier 3: Siamidine 400 mg [Cimetidine 400 mg.] — short intermittent bedtime use if nocturnal symptoms and interaction risk checked
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs in-person eval today if dysphagia/odynophagia, GI bleed/melena, anemia, weight loss, persistent vomiting, chest pain/cardiac concern, severe epigastric pain, age/long duration with cancer risk, or refractory symptoms after correct PPI.

items.Key_Clue: Abnormal discharge; reproductive-age woman; cause unclear
items.Manual_Review_Note: Default triage row; not a drug-only row.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Assess discharge pattern [diagnostic] — curd-like/itch = VC; fishy gray homogeneous = BV; yellow-green frothy = trichomoniasis; mucopurulent/urethral pus = GC/CT risk || If available: vaginal pH + wet prep + KOH whiff + Gram stain/NAAT || If STI risk or mucopurulent discharge → treat/cover GC/CT per STI row
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: If classic VC and low STI risk → vulvovaginal_candidiasis_acute || Tier 1: If fishy gray discharge/pH >4.5 → bacterial_vaginosis_adult || Tier 2: If frothy yellow-green or trichomonad seen → trichomoniasis_vaginal_adult || Tier 3: If discharge + STI risk/PMN high → GC/CT rows
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs in-person eval today if pelvic pain, fever, cervical motion tenderness, pregnancy, severe vulvar lesion/ulcer, postmenopausal bleeding, sexual assault concern, or toxic appearance.

items.Key_Clue: Bristol stool <3, hard stool, painful straining
items.Manual_Review_Note: T.Man-Lax contains stimulant + stool-softener; use as short-course/rescue, not routine long-term.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Lifestyle modification [non-drug] — increase fiber gradually + adequate water || Forlax [Macrogol 4000 10 g.+Saccharin sodium 0.017 g.+Orange-grapefruit flavour 0.15 g.] — 1 sachet PO OD–BID || Mucilin Orange Flavour [Ispaghula Husk 49.0 g./100 g.] — 1 sachet in 150 mL cold water, drink immediately + water chaser OD–TID
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Duphalac 100 [Lactulose 66.7 g/100 ml] — 15–30 mL PO OD–BID; useful if stool softening needed || Tier 2: Milk of Magnesia C.B. [Magnesium Hydroxide 400 mg./5 ml.] — 15–45 mL PO HS/AM; avoid renal impairment || Tier 2: T.Man-Lax [Bisacodyl 5 mg.+Dioctyl sodium sulfosuccinate 15 mg.] — 1–2 tabs PO HS short course if hard stool + poor response || Tier 3: Glycerin Suppositories For Adults [Glycerol (Glycerin) 91 g.+Sodium Stearate 9 g./100 g.] — 1 supp PR PRN if rectal stool/need rapid rectal emptying
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Avoid fiber bulk if severe bloating, suspected obstruction, poor water intake, or fecal impaction without evaluation. Needs eval if alarm features or severe rectal pain/bleeding.

items.Key_Clue: Heavy cyclic bleeding, stable vitals, pregnancy excluded, no red flags
items.Manual_Review_Note: Tranexamic dose not in workbook; keep manual.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Tranxic [Tranexamic Acid 250 mg.] — MANUAL; product exists but workbook instruction N/A; use clinician dose protocol || Ponstan 500 mg [Mefenamic acid 500 mg.] — 1 tab PO TID after food during bleeding/pain if NSAID-safe
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Mefamed 500 [Mefenamic acid 500 mg.] — 1 tab PO TID after food || Tier 1: Meomic 500 [Mefenamic acid 500 mg.] — 1 tab PO TID after food || Tier 2: Cyclic progestin / combined OCP if contraception desired — manual based on contraindications || Tier 3: TVS/SIS/hysteroscopy/endometrial sampling if persistent 3–6 months or risk factors
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Avoid NSAIDs if renal disease, GI bleed/ulcer, anticoagulant high-risk, NSAID allergy. Urgent eval if unstable, pregnancy possible, severe anemia, age >45/risk factors, or persistent AUB.

items.Key_Clue: HTN with CKD, especially albuminuria ≥300 mg/day or ≥300 mg/g Cr
items.Manual_Review_Note: Need UACR/eGFR fields for better engine logic.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Prefer ACEI or ARB first-line if albuminuria and not contraindicated. RX NOW option: Losartan 50 mg (GPO) [Losartan potassium 50 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD; monitor creatinine and potassium after initiation/titration. Add CCB/thiazide if not at target.
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Lanzaar 50 [Losartan potassium 50 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD substitute || Tier 1: Enaril 5 mg [Enalapril Maleate 5 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD if ACEI suitable || Tier 2: Amlodac 5 [Amlodipine 5 mg.] — add if BP uncontrolled or ACEI/ARB contraindicated || Tier 2: GPO - HCTZ 25 [Hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg.] — add if volume/salt-sensitive and kidney function suitable || Tier 3: Refer nephrology if advanced CKD, resistant HTN, rapid eGFR drop, hyperkalemia
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Avoid ACEI+ARB combination. Stop/review ACEI/ARB if pregnancy, angioedema, severe hyperkalemia, AKI, bilateral renal artery stenosis concern.

items.Key_Clue: Very high BP, often >180/110, with or without symptoms
items.Manual_Review_Note: Do not label all high BP as “urgency”; use PSH concept.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Separate PSH from hypertensive emergency. If no acute target-organ damage but mild symptoms: rest, repeat BP, assess triggers, missed meds, pain/anxiety, drugs/substances; adjust oral chronic meds cautiously. Do not rapidly drop BP with IV meds in OPD.
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: If emergency features → hypertension_emergency_er_gate || Tier 1: If missed chronic meds and stable → restart/adjust chronic regimen using hypertension_grade2_or_highrisk_adult || Tier 2: If anxiety/pain trigger → treat trigger + remeasure || Tier 2: Amlodac 5 [Amlodipine 5 mg.] or Losartan 50 mg (GPO) [Losartan potassium 50 mg.] may be used for chronic control, not rapid crash || Tier 3: Follow-up within days, HBPM log
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: ER now if chest pain, dyspnea/pulmonary edema, neuro deficit, confusion, seizure, visual loss, AKI/oliguria, pregnancy severe BP, aortic dissection symptoms, papilledema, or suspected acute organ damage.

items.Key_Clue: Severe BP rise with acute target-organ damage symptoms/signs
items.Manual_Review_Note: Workbook oral products not sufficient for emergency protocol.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: ER/hospital now. Needs IV titratable therapy and organ-specific protocol. OPD drug list not appropriate except safe transfer support.
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Hospital IV protocol depending organ damage || Tier 2: Pregnancy severe preeclampsia/eclampsia → OB emergency protocol || Tier 2: Acute stroke/ACS/aortic dissection/pulmonary edema/AKI → disease-specific emergency pathway || Tier 3: Do not use sublingual/rapid unsupervised BP drop
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: ER now. Avoid rapid uncontrolled BP reduction in clinic; avoid outpatient-only management.

items.Key_Clue: Confirmed office BP 140–159/90–99, no CVD/CKD/TOD, low risk
items.Manual_Review_Note: Price blank if workbook price blank.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Lifestyle modification + HBPM. If BP persists after 3–6 months, start medication. Practical first RX if medication needed: Amlodac 5 [Amlodipine besilate 5 mg. (eq. to Amlodipine 5 mg.)] — 1 tab PO OD. Target initially <140/90 then ≤130/80 if tolerated; avoid SBP <120.
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Ambes 5 mg (GPO) [Amlodipine besylate 5 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD || Tier 1: Norvasc 5 mg [Amlodipine besylate 5 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD || Tier 2: Losartan 50 mg (GPO) [Losartan potassium 50 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD if ACEI cough risk/CKD-DM consideration || Tier 2: Enaril 5 mg [Enalapril Maleate 5 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD; monitor K/Cr || Tier 3: GPO - HCTZ 25 [Hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg.] — 1 tab PO morning if suitable
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Do not use ACEI/ARB in pregnancy or possible pregnancy. Caution amlodipine edema; ACEI cough/angioedema/K/Cr; thiazide gout/hyponatremia.

items.Key_Clue: Confirmed BP ≥160/100 or grade 1 with CVD/CKD/TOD/high CV risk
items.Manual_Review_Note: Check baseline comorbidities before choosing class.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Start medication promptly + lifestyle. Most patients need 2 drugs; prefer ACEI/ARB + CCB or thiazide. RX NOW: Amlodac 5 [Amlodipine besilate 5 mg. (eq. to Amlodipine 5 mg.)] — 1 tab PO OD + Losartan 50 mg (GPO) [Losartan potassium 50 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD. Recheck BP, K, creatinine as appropriate.
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Exforge 5 mg/160 mg [Amlodipine 5 mg.+Valsartan 160 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD if single-pill combo appropriate || Tier 1: Amlodac 5 [Amlodipine 5 mg.] + Enaril 5 mg [Enalapril Maleate 5 mg.] — 1 tab each PO OD, monitor K/Cr || Tier 2: Amlodac 5 + GPO - HCTZ 25 [Hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg.] — 1 tab each PO OD/morning || Tier 2: Losartan 50 mg (GPO) + GPO - HCTZ 25 — if CCB edema/intolerance || Tier 3: Concor 2.5 mg [Bisoprolol fumarate 2.5 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD if CAD/HF/rate control indication, not routine first pick
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Avoid ACEI+ARB combination. Avoid ACEI/ARB in pregnancy. ER now if hypertensive emergency symptoms or acute target-organ damage.

items.Key_Clue: Office BP elevated; diagnosis not yet confirmed
items.Manual_Review_Note: Use as triage row before chronic HTN RX.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Confirm BP correctly. Repeat office BP and/or HBPM: morning + evening, 2 readings each episode, 1 min apart, for 3–7 days. Lifestyle advice: salt reduction, weight control, exercise, limit alcohol, stop smoking. No immediate drug if not confirmed and no red flags.
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: If office BP 140–159/90–99 and low risk → hypertension_grade1_lowrisk_adult || Tier 1: If ≥160/100 or high risk/CVD/CKD/TOD → hypertension_grade2_or_highrisk_adult || Tier 2: If home average ≥135/85 → treat/confirm as hypertension || Tier 3: If pregnancy → hypertension_pregnancy_gate
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: ER now if chest pain, neuro deficit, dyspnea/pulmonary edema, severe headache with neuro sign, visual loss, confusion, seizure, AKI/oliguria, aortic dissection symptoms, or BP crisis symptoms.

items.Key_Clue: Pregnant, missed period, or reproductive-age patient with possible pregnancy
items.Manual_Review_Note: Workbook lacks methyldopa/labetalol and IV emergency products.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Do not use ACEI/ARB/DRI. Severe BP ≥160 systolic and/or ≥110 diastolic needs urgent treatment and OB evaluation. Workbook-supported possible oral option: Nelapine 20 SR [Nifedipine 20 mg.] — specialist/OB-directed only. Cesoline W [Hydralazine HCl 25 mg.] is oral hydralazine, not substitute for IV emergency protocol.
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Manual methyldopa — missing product || Tier 1: Manual labetalol — missing product || Tier 1: Nelapine 20 SR [Nifedipine 20 mg.] — OB-directed oral option only || Tier 2: Cesoline W [Hydralazine HCl 25 mg.] — not for unsupervised emergency use || Tier 3: IV hydralazine/labetalol/nifedipine IR protocols — hospital only
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs same-day OB/ER if BP ≥160/110, headache, visual symptoms, RUQ/epigastric pain, dyspnea, edema with symptoms, seizure, proteinuria concern, or reduced fetal movement.

items.Key_Clue: CKD with urine albumin ≥300 mg/day or ≥300 mg/g creatinine
items.Manual_Review_Note: Albuminuria <300 may use any class; albuminuria ≥300 prefers ACEI/ARB.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: ACEI/ARB first-line class [drug] — Losartan 50 mg (GPO) [Losartan potassium 50 mg.] 1 tab PO OD OR Enaril 5 mg [Enalapril Maleate 5 mg.] 1–2 tabs PO OD if ACEI suitable || Lifestyle/low sodium [non-drug] || Monitor renal function/K+ [lab]
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Candesartan option: Blopress 8 mg [Candesartan cilexetil 8 mg.] 1 tab PO OD after dinner || Tier 1: Valsartan option: Diovan 80 mg [Valsartan 80 mg.] 1 tab PO OD || Tier 2: Add CCB: Ambes 5 mg (GPO) [Amlodipine besylate 5 mg.] 1 tab PO OD || Tier 3: Diuretic adjustment per CKD stage/manual review
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Do not combine ACEI + ARB. Monitor creatinine/eGFR and potassium. Avoid NSAIDs.

items.Key_Clue: HT with DM; higher CV/renal risk
items.Manual_Review_Note: Target differs by age; 18–65 often 120–130/70–79 if tolerated, older 130–139/70–79.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Lifestyle modification [non-drug] || ACEI/ARB-based regimen [drug] — Losartan 50 mg (GPO) [Losartan potassium 50 mg.] 1 tab PO OD OR Enaril 5 mg [Enalapril Maleate 5 mg.] 1–2 tabs PO OD if ACEI suitable || Add CCB if needed — Ambes 5 mg (GPO) [Amlodipine besylate 5 mg.] 1 tab PO OD
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: ARB substitute: Blopress 8 mg [Candesartan cilexetil 8 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD after dinner || Tier 1: Valsartan option: Diovan 80 mg [Valsartan 80 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD || Tier 2: If uncontrolled → combine ACEI/ARB + CCB or diuretic || Tier 3: Statin risk review: Atorvastatin Sandoz 10–20 mg [Atorvastatin] physician-directed
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Do not combine ACEI + ARB. Avoid ACEI/ARB in pregnancy. Monitor renal function/K+.

items.Disease_Name: Essential hypertension grade 1 with high CV risk
items.Key_Clue: Grade 1 BP plus DM, CKD, CVD, TOD, or Thai CV risk >10%
items.Manual_Review_Note: Use patient-specific comorbidity/contraindication selection.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Lifestyle modification [non-drug] — same for all HT || Start 1–2 drug therapy after diagnosis [drug] — Ambes 5 mg (GPO) [Amlodipine besylate 5 mg.] 1 tab PO OD +/− Losartan 50 mg (GPO) [Losartan potassium 50 mg.] 1 tab PO OD if combination appropriate || Risk reduction [non-drug] — smoking cessation, lipid/CV risk review
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Amlodac 5 [Amlodipine besilate 5 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD substitute || Tier 1: Loranta 50 mg [Losartan potassium 50 mg.] — physician-directed ARB substitute || Tier 2: Enaril 5 mg [Enalapril Maleate 5 mg.] — 1–2 tabs PO OD if ACEI suitable || Tier 2: GPO - HCTZ 25 [Hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD if diuretic suitable || Tier 3: Blopress 8 mg [Candesartan cilexetil 8 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD after dinner if ARB substitute needed
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Do not combine ACEI + ARB. Avoid ACEI/ARB/DRI in pregnancy or possible pregnancy. ER if emergency symptoms.

items.Key_Clue: Office BP 140–159/90–99, no CVD/DM/CKD/TOD/high CV risk
items.Manual_Review_Note: Default row for low-risk grade 1; drug only after persistence/diagnosis.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Lifestyle modification [non-drug] — low sodium, weight loss if overweight, aerobic exercise ≥5 days/week, limit alcohol, stop smoking || HBPM follow-up [monitoring] — target home BP <135/85; confirm persistence || Review BP-raising drugs [safety] — NSAIDs, decongestants, steroids, stimulants, OCP, herbal stimulants
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: If persistent after 3–6 months → Ambes 5 mg (GPO) [Amlodipine besylate 5 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD || Tier 1: Losartan 50 mg (GPO) [Losartan potassium 50 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD if ARB preferred || Tier 2: Enaril 5 mg [Enalapril Maleate 5 mg.] — 1–2 tabs PO OD if ACEI suitable || Tier 3: HCTZ 25 [Hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg.] — 1 tab PO morning if diuretic suitable
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs in-person eval today if repeated BP ≥180/110 or any hypertensive emergency symptom; avoid NSAIDs if possible.

items.Key_Clue: Office BP 160–179/100–109 after proper measurement/diagnosis
items.Manual_Review_Note: Combination therapy should be individualized; Exforge is workbook product but not automatic for all.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Lifestyle modification [non-drug] || Start pharmacologic therapy after diagnosis [drug] — Ambes 5 mg (GPO) [Amlodipine besylate 5 mg.] 1 tab PO OD + Losartan 50 mg (GPO) [Losartan potassium 50 mg.] 1 tab PO OD if no contraindication; aim control within 3 months

items.Key_Clue: Confirmed/suspected malaria with severe signs or high parasitemia
items.Manual_Review_Note: No OPD RX except safe supportive antipyretic while transferring.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: ER/hospital treatment. Guideline first-line: IV artesunate 2.4 mg/kg IV bolus at 0, 12, 24 hr then daily until oral ACT possible; if BW <20 kg use 3 mg/kg. Workbook lacks IV artesunate and ACT products. Supportive OPD only if awaiting transfer: paracetamol for fever and urgent transfer.
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: IV artesunate — manual/hospital only || Tier 2: If no IV artesunate → quinine dihydrochloride IV infusion, not bolus; manual/hospital only || Tier 3: Once able PO → ACT 3 days + primaquine if not contraindicated || Tier 3: Evaluate co-infection and complications; monitor glucose, renal, liver, anemia, acidosis
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: ER now. Do not manage severe malaria as outpatient. Avoid quinine bolus; monitor hypoglycemia/cardiotoxicity. Avoid doxycycline in pregnancy, breastfeeding, and children <8 years.

Supportive only while referring/testing: Tylenol 500 mg strip 10s [Paracetamol 500 mg.] — 1 tab PO q6 hr PRN fever.

items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs same-day test/referral. ER now if altered consciousness, seizure, shock, dyspnea, jaundice, oliguria, dark urine, severe anemia, bleeding, hypoglycemia, acidosis, pulmonary edema, pregnancy, infant, or suspected P. knowlesi severe course.

items.Key_Clue: Lab-confirmed malaria without severe features
items.Manual_Review_Note: Manual treatment row only; antimalarial products absent.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Treatment must follow species + area resistance + G6PD + pregnancy/lactation. Workbook lacks chloroquine, primaquine, tafenoquine, DHA-piperaquine, artesunate-pyronaridine, artesunate IV, quinine. Do not auto-prescribe; refer/obtain official malaria drug supply.
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: P. falciparum non-resistant area → DHA-piperaquine 3 days + primaquine 0.5 mg/kg once; manual missing products || Tier 1: P. falciparum Sisaket/Ubon resistance area → artesunate-pyronaridine 3 days + primaquine once; manual missing products || Tier 1: P. vivax → chloroquine 3 days + radical cure based on G6PD: tafenoquine single dose or primaquine 7/14 days; manual missing products || Tier 2: P. ovale → chloroquine 3 days + primaquine 14 days; no tafenoquine || Tier 2: P. malariae/P. knowlesi → chloroquine 3 days; consider DHA-piperaquine for knowlesi || Tier 3: mixed infections follow falciparum-based ACT + primaquine rules
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Hard stop: pregnancy = no primaquine/tafenoquine. Tafenoquine only if P. vivax monoinfection, age ≥16, BW ≥35 kg, quantitative G6PD normal ≥6.1 U/g Hb; avoid breastfeeding and metformin/biguanide.

Workbook-supported option: Spornar [Itraconazole 100 mg.] — continuous 200 mg/day: 2 caps PO OD after food 6 weeks fingernail / 12 weeks toenail OR pulse 200 mg BID 1 week/month: 2 pulses fingernail / 3 pulses toenail. Consider baseline LFT and interaction check.
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Terbinafine 250 mg PO OD — manual missing product; preferred for dermatophyte nail fungus || Tier 1: Spornar [Itraconazole 100 mg.] — 2 caps PO OD after food continuous course OR 2 caps PO BID pulse regimen || Tier 2: Funa 200 mg [Fluconazole 200 mg.] — 1 cap PO weekly, 3–9 months fingernail / 6–18 months toenail if terbinafine/itraconazole unsuitable || Tier 2: Biozole 150 mg [Fluconazole 150 mg.] — 1 cap PO weekly alternative dose band || Tier 3: Combine oral + topical lacquer + debridement for poor prognostic features/treatment failure
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Avoid itraconazole in heart failure/ventricular dysfunction. Check CYP3A4 interactions, especially statins/digoxin. Avoid systemic antifungal in pregnancy unless specialist-directed. Refer if treatment failure after standard therapy, suspected resistant mold, liver disease, CKD, or polypharmacy.

items.Key_Clue: Still symptomatic after adequate lifestyle + conventional laxatives with good compliance
items.Manual_Review_Note: New agents not proven superior to conventional laxatives and are cost/availability dependent.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Optimize compliance [non-drug] — confirm water/fiber/toileting routine and correct use || Optimize conventional laxative [drug] — PEG/lactulose/MOM ± short-course stimulant with different mechanism || Refer GI/colorectal [diagnostic] — anorectal physiologic tests if poor response
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Forlax [Macrogol 4000 10 g.+Saccharin sodium 0.017 g.+Orange-grapefruit flavour 0.15 g.] — optimize OD–BID || Tier 1: Add Dulcolax [Bisacodyl 5 mg.] short rescue if osmotic alone inadequate || Tier 2: Evaluate defecation disorder: DRE, balloon expulsion test, anorectal manometry || Tier 3: New agents only after conventional failure/limitation and reassess at 2 weeks; stop and refer if no response
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs specialist evaluation; urgent if alarm features, severe pain/distension/vomiting, GI bleed, suspected obstruction, or progressive new constipation.

items.Key_Clue: Still sleepy after apparently adequate PAP use and residual AHI controlled
items.Manual_Review_Note: Modafinil/armodafinil not recommended as routine Thai guideline patch; specialist-only if truly residual sleepiness after adequate PAP.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Reassess PAP and sleep [diagnostic] — verify adherence, residual AHI/RDI, leak, sleep duration, sedating meds, depression, hypothyroid/medical causes || Specialist referral [sleep] — consider MSLT/MWT if adequate PAP but severe persistent sleepiness || Safety advice [non-drug] — avoid drowsy driving/high-risk work until controlled
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Optimize PAP/device issues first || Tier 2: Evaluate other sleep disorders such as narcolepsy/insomnia/circadian disorder || Tier 3: Wake-promoting agents only by specialist; not auto-RX from OPD short input
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs urgent safety restriction if drowsy driving, high-risk occupation, accidents/near-misses, or severe functional impairment.

items.Key_Clue: Uncontrolled BP despite lifestyle + 3 appropriate antihypertensives including diuretic
items.Manual_Review_Note: Resistant HT row should not be first-line.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Check white-coat effect/HBPM [diagnostic] || Check adherence and BP-raising drugs [safety] || Evaluate secondary HT [diagnostic] || If true resistant and already on 3 drugs incl diuretic → Altone [Spironolactone 25 mg.] 1 tab PO morning per clinician review
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Optimize diuretic and regimen adherence || Tier 2: Add spironolactone if not already used and K+/renal function safe || Tier 2: If not suitable, beta-blocker option e.g. Concor 2.5 mg [Bisoprolol fumarate 2.5 mg.] || Tier 3: Alpha-blocker option e.g. Cardura XL 4 mg [Doxazosin 4 mg.] specialist/manual review
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs specialist/secondary-cause review. Avoid spironolactone if hyperkalemia/significant renal impairment. Avoid NSAIDs/stimulants.

items.Key_Clue: New/worsening dysmenorrhea after years of painless menses; pelvic pathology symptoms
items.Manual_Review_Note: Investigation-first row.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Do not treat as simple primary dysmenorrhea only. Evaluate for endometriosis, adenomyosis, myoma, polyp, PID, ectopic pregnancy/miscarriage, obstruction, IBS/IBD/interstitial cystitis || Pregnancy test if pregnancy possible || Pelvic exam/US if indicated
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: If cyclic pelvic pain + dyspareunia/chronic pelvic pain → endometriosis_pain_suspected || Tier 2: If heavy bleeding + dysmenorrhea → abnormal_uterine_bleeding_triage / heavy_menstrual_bleeding_stable || Tier 3: If fever/discharge/pelvic tenderness → PID/STI evaluation row
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: ER now if pregnancy/ectopic concern, sudden severe pain, syncope, fever/toxic, acute abdomen, heavy bleeding, severe anemia, or adnexal torsion concern.

items.Key_Clue: TG ≥500 mg/dL; pancreatitis risk
items.Manual_Review_Note: Needs TG repeat and renal/liver monitoring.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Stop alcohol + reduce refined carbohydrate/simple sugar + weight control + review secondary causes/medications || Supralip NT 145 mg [Fenofibrate 145 mg.] — 1 tab PO OD with food if renal function appropriate
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Fibril 160 [Fenofibrate 160 mg.] — 1 cap PO OD with food substitute || Tier 2: Hidil 600 mg [Gemfibrozil 600 mg.] — 1 tab PO BID before meals if not on statin and fenofibrate unsuitable || Tier 2: Hidil 300 mg [Gemfibrozil 300 mg.] — 1–2 caps PO BID before meals if not on statin || Tier 3: Omega-3 EPA/DHA 2–4 g/day — manual; product not found in workbook
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Do not combine gemfibrozil with statin. Fenofibrate caution CKD; avoid if severe renal impairment. ER if severe epigastric pain/vomiting/pancreatitis concern.

items.Key_Clue: Loud snoring but low OSA risk; STOP-Bang <3 and no strong clinical/comorbidity concern
items.Manual_Review_Note: This is not a drug-first disease. Avoid sedatives for sleep/snoring.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Conservative treatment [non-drug] — sleep hygiene, adequate sleep ≥7 h/night, avoid alcohol/sedatives/muscle relaxants before sleep, weight reduction if overweight, exercise || Risk review [screening] — check BP, BMI, neck circumference, daytime sleepiness, witnessed apnea, choking/gasping || Consider sleep test [diagnostic] — if uncertainty, high-risk occupation, severe symptoms, or patient anxiety
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Positional therapy [non-drug] — avoid supine sleep if positional snoring || Tier 2: Oral appliance referral [device] — consider adjustable MAD for bothersome snoring/low-risk OSA pathway || Tier 2: ENT evaluation [procedure] — if nasal obstruction, tonsil/palate abnormality, or severe social snoring || Tier 3: Sleep specialist referral — if unclear diagnosis or worsening symptoms
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs sleep/ENT eval if witnessed apnea, excessive daytime sleepiness, drowsy driving, refractory HTN, heart failure/CAD/arrhythmia/stroke, severe obesity, high-risk occupation, or suspected central/complex sleep disorder.

items.Disease_Name: Sudden sensorineural hearing loss / idiopathic sudden hearing loss
items.Key_Clue: Sudden unilateral hearing loss within 72h ± tinnitus/vertigo; normal otoscopy or no conductive cause
items.Manual_Review_Note: Need real oral steroid product/BDS/price before active RX; guideline is adult ≥18, no pediatric regimen from this source
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: URGENT ENT/audiometry [procedure] — distinguish CHL vs SNHL; audiometry ASAP, target within 14 days || ADD_PRODUCT_REQUIRED: Prednisone/Prednisolone oral corticosteroid [not in workbook] — prednisone 1 mg/kg/day PO OD, usual max 60 mg/day, 10–14 days then taper OR equivalent prednisolone regimen; best within 2 weeks of onset; use shared decision-making || Counsel: avoid noise, explain uncertain recovery, follow-up audiometry at end of treatment and within 6 months
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: If systemic steroid contraindicated or high-risk DM/HTN/ulcer/psych risk → urgent ENT for intratympanic steroid discussion || Tier 2: Incomplete recovery 2–6 weeks after onset → offer/refer for intratympanic steroid salvage || Tier 3: HBOT + steroid may be considered within 2 weeks initial or within 1 month salvage if available
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: ER now if focal neuro deficit, severe vertigo/ataxia, dysarthria, diplopia, facial weakness/numbness, severe headache, bilateral sudden hearing loss, recurrent episodes, head trauma, barotrauma, ototoxic exposure, or suspected stroke. Do NOT routine CT/labs/antiviral/vasodilator/vasoactive drugs.

items.Key_Clue: Loud habitual snoring + witnessed apnea/gasping/daytime sleepiness or STOP-Bang ≥3 or major comorbidity
items.Manual_Review_Note: Do not prescribe stimulant or sleeping pills from short OPD input. Diagnosis requires sleep evaluation/testing.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Sleep test referral [diagnostic] — attended PSG or suitable unattended sleep test/HSAT depending on comorbidity/resources || Conservative treatment [non-drug] — weight reduction if overweight, avoid alcohol/sedatives before sleep, sleep hygiene, avoid sleep deprivation || Safety advice [non-drug] — avoid drowsy driving/high-risk duty until evaluated if severe sleepiness
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: If no significant comorbidity → attended or unattended sleep test acceptable if proper device/interpretation || Tier 1: If significant comorbidity → prefer in-hospital/attended pathway or specialist-directed testing || Tier 2: Treat nasal obstruction/rhinitis if present to improve sleep/PAP tolerance || Tier 3: Specialist referral if negative test but high clinical suspicion
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: ER/urgent eval if severe daytime somnolence with accident risk, unstable cardiopulmonary disease, severe hypoxemia, acute heart failure, stroke symptoms, or opioid/sedative respiratory depression concern.

items.Key_Clue: Adult T2DM, stable OPD, not pregnant, no DKA/HHS, no severe symptoms
items.Manual_Review_Note: Metformin first-line only if eGFR and contraindications allow.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Lifestyle modification [non-drug] — diabetes education, diet, exercise, weight/waist control || Metformin Hydrochloride 500 mg (GPO) [Metformin HCl 500 mg.] — start 1 tab PO after meal OD-BID; titrate per clinician/tolerance || Baseline assessment [monitoring] — A1C, FPG, lipid, Cr/eGFR, urine albumin, BP, eye/foot/oral health
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Glucophage 500 mg [Metformin HCI 500 mg.] — same role, brand substitute || Tier 1: Siamformet 500 mg [Metformin HCl 500 mg.] — same role, substitute || Tier 2: Glucophage XR 750 mg [Metformin HCl 750 mg.] — once daily after dinner if GI intolerance/adherence issue || Tier 3: If metformin contraindicated/intolerant → Galvus 50 mg [Vildagliptin 50 mg.] manual clinician-directed option
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs in-person evaluation if FPG >300 mg/dL, A1C >11%, ketosis/DKA/HHS symptom, marked weight loss, pregnancy, age/phenotype suggests T1DM.

items.Disease_Name: Type 2 diabetes mellitus, not controlled on metformin
items.Key_Clue: Known T2DM; A1C/FPG above target despite lifestyle + metformin
items.Manual_Review_Note: SU dosing should be individualized; avoid glibenclamide in elderly/CKD risk.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Continue lifestyle + metformin if tolerated [drug] || Add sulfonylurea if suitable [drug] — Glizide [Glipizide 5 mg.] 1/2–1 tab PO before breakfast OD per clinician OR Diamicron MR 60 mg [Gliclazide 60 mg.] 1 tab PO before breakfast per clinician
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Glipizide substitute — Glycediab 5 mg [Glipizide 5 mg.] 1/2–1 tab PO before breakfast || Tier 1: Minidiab [Glipizide 5 mg.] 1/2–1 tab PO before breakfast/lunch || Tier 2: Galvus 50 mg [Vildagliptin 50 mg.] 1 tab PO BID if hypoglycemia avoidance needed / SU unsuitable || Tier 3: Amaryl 2 mg [Glimepiride 2 mg.] clinician-directed; start low especially CKD/elderly
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Need specialist/insulin review if FPG >300, A1C >11, symptomatic catabolism, recurrent hypoglycemia, CKD stage 4–5, pregnancy, or suspected T1DM.

items.Key_Clue: FPG >300 mg/dL or A1C >11% or symptomatic hyperglycemia
items.Manual_Review_Note: Do not force oral-only OPD regimen.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Referral / insulin-start gate [safety] — evaluate for DKA/HHS, dehydration, infection, weight loss, T1DM/LADA; insulin may be required || Continue safe hydration and urgent clinical assessment
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: If stable but very high sugar → same-day physician evaluation for insulin/combination therapy || Tier 2: If symptomatic or catabolic → urgent referral || Tier 3: Workbook lacks verified insulin product rows; keep insulin protocol manual/specialist
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: ER now if vomiting, dehydration, confusion, abdominal pain, Kussmaul breathing, ketone positive, severe infection, hypotension, or pregnancy.

items.Key_Clue: Tinnitus ≥6 months causing sleep, concentration, mood, communication, or quality-of-life impairment
items.Manual_Review_Note: Medication is not RX NOW. CBT is recommended; sound therapy is optional; hearing aids if hearing loss.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Comprehensive audiologic examination [investigation] — characterize hearing loss and asymmetry || CBT referral [non-drug] — recommended for persistent bothersome tinnitus to improve coping/QOL || Education/counseling [non-drug] — management strategies, natural history, no proven cure, avoid unproven meds/supplements || Hearing aid evaluation [device/referral] — if documented hearing loss
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Hearing aid evaluation if any documented hearing loss || Tier 2: Sound therapy / environmental sound / tinnitus device may be offered || Tier 3: Mental health referral if severe anxiety/depression; ENT/neurotology if unilateral/asymmetric/pulsatile/neurologic features
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs in-person eval today if suicidal ideation, severe depression/anxiety, sudden hearing loss, pulsatile tinnitus, unilateral tinnitus with hearing loss, focal neuro signs, vestibular symptoms, or suspected secondary tinnitus.

items.Key_Clue: Ringing/buzzing/roaring/clicking sound without external source; first exclude secondary cause
items.Manual_Review_Note: Do not use betahistine, dimenhydrinate, amitriptyline, gabapentin, pregabalin, benzodiazepines, intratympanic steroids, Ginkgo, melatonin, zinc, or vitamins as routine primary tinnitus treatment.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Targeted ear + neuro history/exam [non-drug] — onset, duration, laterality, pulsatile quality, hearing loss, vertigo, ototoxic meds, noise exposure, depression/anxiety, otoscopy || Audiology referral [investigation] — prompt if unilateral, persistent ≥6 months, or hearing difficulty; may obtain for any tinnitus || Education/counseling [non-drug] — explain symptom not disease, protect from loud noise, avoid false-cure meds/supplements, follow-up if persistent/worse
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: If nonbothersome and no red flags → reassure + education + follow-up PRN || Tier 2: If persistent bothersome ≥6 months → CBT + audiology pathway || Tier 3: If hearing loss documented → hearing aid evaluation; sound therapy may be offered
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs in-person eval today / ENT-audiology if sudden hearing loss, unilateral tinnitus, pulsatile tinnitus, asymmetric hearing loss, focal neuro deficit, vertigo, otorrhea, visible middle ear mass, severe anxiety/depression, suicidal ideation. Avoid routine imaging/drugs in uncomplicated bilateral nonpulsatile tinnitus.

items.Disease_Name: Unilateral or pulsatile tinnitus / possible secondary tinnitus
items.Key_Clue: One-sided tinnitus, pulse-synchronous tinnitus, asymmetric hearing loss, focal neurologic sign, or objective tinnitus
items.Manual_Review_Note: Keep separate from primary tinnitus. Imaging is not routine; it is red-flag/secondary-cause driven.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: ENT/audiology referral [referral] — prompt assessment for secondary tinnitus || Comprehensive audiology [investigation] — evaluate asymmetric SNHL/conductive loss || Imaging decision [investigation] — consider MRI/IAC or vascular imaging only if unilateral/asymmetric/pulsatile/neuro sign per specialist pathway
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Treat visible/treatable cause if found, e.g., cerumen, otitis externa/media, ETD, middle ear disease || Tier 2: If sudden SNHL → follow SSNHL urgent pathway, not tinnitus pathway || Tier 3: If psychiatric auditory hallucination/psychosis concern → mental health pathway
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: ER now if sudden neuro deficit, stroke symptoms, severe headache, acute sudden hearing loss, suicidal ideation, or pulsatile tinnitus with neurologic signs. Do not treat as simple tinnitus.

items.Key_Clue: Yellow-green frothy discharge, vaginal erythema, strawberry cervix, motile trichomonad, pH >4.5
items.Manual_Review_Note: Follow-up wet smear at 7 days and 3 months per source.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Metronidazole 400 [Metronidazole 400 mg.] — 5 tabs PO once for 2 g single dose OR 1 tab PO BID x 5–7 days || Treat current sexual partner(s) same regimen || Abstain from sex until patient and partner treated
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Metrolex 400 mg [Metronidazole 400 mg.] — same regimen substitute || Tier 2: Gyno Gena-500 [Tinidazole 500 mg.] — 4 tabs PO once, not pregnancy/lactation || Tier 2: Asiazole-T.N. tabslets [Tinidazole 500 mg.] — 4 tabs PO once, not pregnancy/lactation || Tier 3: Persistent confirmed TV → metronidazole repeat/intensified regimen, manual
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: No alcohol with metronidazole/tinidazole; avoid tinidazole in pregnancy/lactation; STI screen and partner treatment needed; evaluate if pelvic pain/fever.

items.Key_Clue: Widespread severe wheals/itch, no airway compromise, no hypotension, no systemic anaphylaxis
items.Use_When: Severe acute urticaria flare after red flags excluded
items.Manual_Review_Note: drowsy, dry mouth
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Zyrtec [Cetirizine dihydrochloride 10 mg.]
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: 1 tab PO OD

items.Key_Clue: Itch/burning, curd-like discharge, pH usually <4.5; no strong STI signs
items.Manual_Review_Note: Use vaginal clotrimazole in pregnancy/lactation instead of oral azole.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Biozole 150 mg [Fluconazole 150 mg.] — 1 cap PO once after food || OR Canesten Vaginal tabs. 1s [Clotrimazole 500 mg.] — insert 1 tab PV HS once || Avoid irritants/douching/tight clothes; partner treatment not needed
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Gynosten-500 [Clotrimazole 500 mg.] — insert 1 tab PV HS once || Tier 1: Canesten Vaginal tabs. 6s [Clotrimazole 100 mg.] — insert 1 tab PV HS x 6 nights || Tier 2: Funa 200 mg [Fluconazole 200 mg.] — 1 cap PO once if 150–200 mg regimen chosen || Tier 3: Spornar [Itraconazole 100 mg.] — 2 caps PO BID x 1 day, manual only
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Avoid oral fluconazole/itraconazole in pregnancy or breastfeeding; evaluate if recurrent, severe vulvar swelling/ulcer, pelvic pain, fever, STI risk, or no response.

items.Key_Clue: ≥4 symptomatic VC episodes/year or persistent/recurrent symptoms
items.Manual_Review_Note: Do not auto-dispense 6-month regimen without clinician review.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Confirm diagnosis/culture if possible [diagnostic] || Induction: fluconazole 100–200 mg PO every 3 days x 3 doses, then weekly x 6 months; manual clinician-directed || Consider diabetes control, pregnancy, steroid/antibiotic exposure, non-albicans Candida
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: Biozole 150 mg [Fluconazole 150 mg.] — use as available strength for recurrent protocol, manual || Tier 2: Canesten Vaginal tabs. 6s [Clotrimazole 100 mg.] — nightly x 14 days then 500 mg weekly x 6 months, manual || Tier 3: Vagicin [Nystatin 100,000 units.+Chloramphenicol 250 mg.+Diiodohydroxyguin 100 mg.] — not default; consider only if non-albicans/manual review
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Needs specialist/gyne eval if recurrent, treatment failure, pregnancy, immunocompromise, uncontrolled diabetes, ulcer/erosion, or diagnosis uncertain.

items.Key_Clue: Office BP ≥140/90 but home BP <135/85
items.Manual_Review_Note: Monitoring/risk row, not routine RX.
items.Legacy_RX_NOW: Lifestyle modification [non-drug] || Annual office + home BP follow-up [monitoring] || CV risk/TOD assessment [diagnostic]
items.Legacy_SWAPS: Tier 1: No antihypertensive for all white-coat HT || Tier 2: Consider drug only if high/very high CV risk or TOD || Tier 3: Use HBPM/ABPM if discordant readings persist
items.AVOID_RED_FLAGS: Do not over-treat if home BP low; avoid home BP <120/80 from unnecessary medication.
